Dear tUME user,                                         January 22, 1993

Here is a quick tour of some of the key features of tUME.

EQUIPMENT NEEDED

You will need an IBM PC, preferably 386 or faster, with 640K main
memory. To follow this example, you will also need approximately
256K of EMS 4.0 or XMS 2.0 memory.

INSTALLATION

Please refer to PACKLIST.TXT for installation instructions.

GETTING STARTED

To start tUME, type DEMOTUME at the DOS prompt.

To load in a sample map, select Project|Load... from the menus. Open
the sub-directory EXAMPLES by double-clicking on it, then double click
on the file 3LAYER.MAP. tUME will load in the 3 Layer Room, and the
associate ILBM files that contain the tilesets (SIMPLE.LBM, COLLIDE.LBM,
and OBJECTS.LBM).

BASIC OPERATIONS

Press 'b' to enter the brush mode, and drag-select the vertical green
cylinder with the left mouse button. Now subsequent presses of the left
mouse button will stamp the cylinder, right mouse button presses will
erase the area underneath the cylinder.

SELECTING TILES FROM SOURCE ROOM

Press <SPACE> once, and you should be viewing the tileset SIMPLE.LBM.
Drag-select with the left mouse button the square with four screw in
it. An outline should appear around the box. Press <SPACE> to return
to the 3 Layer Room, and press the left mouse button to stamp the box.

OPERATING WITH LAYERS

At the very right of the title bar it says 03 01vu. The 03 means there
are three layers total, the 01 means you are currently editing the
first layer. Press <ALT>-<UP ARROW> to up layers, <ALT>-<DOWN ARROW>
to move down layers. Move to the second layer; the display should read
03 02vu.

DISPLAYING ONLY SECOND LAYER

Select View|ShowOnlyFloor from the menus, and tUME will display only
the second layer (the contour layer).

ADDING CONTOUR TO THE SECOND LAYER

Press <SPACE> once to switch to the source pane, and press '2' to cycle
to the room with the tilesets COLLIDE.LBM and OBJECTS.LBM. Click with
the left mouse button on the tile that looks like a forward slash (/),
the press <SPACE> to return to the 3 Layer Room. Now when you press
the left mouse button, it will draw the '/', and the right mouse button
will erase tiles.

SHOW ALL LAYERS AGAIN

Select View|ShowOnlyFloor to make all layers visible again.

ZOOM IN AND OUT

Press '<' (<SHIFT>-',') to zoom out one step, or '>' (<SHIFT>-'.') to
zoom in one step. Or press <ALT>-',' to zoom out the maximum amount,
or press <ALT>-'.' to zoom in the maximum amount. Press 'm' to toggle
the zoom mode on and off. Note that all editing features are available
at all zoom settings

PALETTE EDITOR

Press 'p' to bring up the palette editor.

THAT'S IT!

You have now seen the basic features of tUME. Feel free to try some
of tUME's other features.
